title: Lock v0.3 distribution release state
reason: Lock the v0.3 distribution and release state after README, release binary, curl installer, Homebrew, manual release, and post-release roadmap planning work, without claiming unavailable install paths or adding runtime behavior.
proposed_changes: Record that README.md and README.ko.md are public-facing visual pamphlets backed by docs rather than runtime behavior.
Lock source/local binary availability as factual while keeping hosted release binaries and curl installer release-gated until real GitHub Release assets and checksums exist and are verified.

Lock Homebrew as planned with no published tap or formula, no-terminal mode as assisted unless trusted CLI proof is supplied, model workspace packs as UX rather than authority, and ni-kernel as pre-runtime.
risk_impact: Strengthens the existing high-severity packaging-claim risk by adding explicit release binary, curl installer, Homebrew, no-terminal, model-pack, and pre-runtime boundary wording.
readiness_impact: Expected to remain READY because CAP-017 keeps accepted requirements, evaluations, mitigated high risks, artifacts, non-goals, and no blocker questions while adding EVAL-021 and ART-043 traceability.
